obligation: EHR systems include a European logging software component.
derived_requirement: [INTERPRETATION] Treat TrustEvidence as a separable evidence/logging layer, not as a clinical-payload repository.
mechanism: interface specification envelope and backend receipt; IG validation emitter/verifier CapabilityStatements; A1/A2/A3 backend separation.
verification_test: TE-LEGAL-01: access event emits artefact plus backend receipt without storing payload.

obligation: EHR systems used by health professionals provide reliable identification and authentication mechanisms.
derived_requirement: [INTERPRETATION] Evidence emission binds artefacts to authenticated actor and signer context.
mechanism: interface specification emitter and signature fields; IG validation AuditEvent.agent; formal review signer-authorisation repair.
verification_test: TE-LEGAL-02: reject missing or unauthorised actor/signer binding.

obligation: Access logs record the healthcare provider or other individuals having accessed personal electronic health data.
derived_requirement: [INTERPRETATION] Access attestations carry actor/provider evidence sufficient for later v2.0.0 development.
mechanism: interface specification emitter organisation token; IG validation AuditEvent.agent; BALP/ATNA actor-accountability mapping.
verification_test: TE-LEGAL-03: W1 access artefact includes actor, role and organisation token.

obligation: Access logs record the specific natural person or persons whose data were accessed.
derived_requirement: [INTERPRETATION] Store tokenised subject reference in the evidence layer and leave re-identification to authorised custody.
mechanism: interface specification subject_ref and privacy_controls; IG validation AuditEvent.entity; off-chain payload custody.
verification_test: TE-LEGAL-04: backend-visible artefacts contain subject token, not direct identifier.

obligation: Access logs record categories of data accessed.
derived_requirement: [INTERPRETATION] Evidence includes controlled data-category code aligned to EHDS priority categories or local extensions.
mechanism: interface specification semantic_binding and payload commitment context; IG validation terminology and entity detail mapping.
verification_test: TE-LEGAL-05: data category is present and drawn from controlled value set.

obligation: Access logs record the time and date of access.
derived_requirement: [INTERPRETATION] Distinguish operational event time, emission time and backend receipt/checkpoint time.
mechanism: interface specification occurred_at, emitted_at, time_source and receipt issued_at; formal definitions/formal review freshness theorem.
verification_test: TE-LEGAL-06: reject missing time source, stale checkpoint or timestamp-skew violation.

obligation: Access logs record origin or origins of data.
derived_requirement: [INTERPRETATION] Evidence binds source system or data-origin token without copying payload.
mechanism: interface specification semantic_binding and payload_binding reference token; IG validation AuditEvent.source and Provenance entity mapping.
verification_test: TE-LEGAL-07: source/origin token is present and tied to commitment context.

obligation: Harmonised components include tools or mechanisms to review and analyse log data, or support external software for that purpose.
derived_requirement: [INTERPRETATION] The verifier API and query layer become evaluation obligations, not optional conveniences.
mechanism: interface specification verification API; external-service integration harness; measurement protocol audit-query metric; external benchmark run requires external validation log log operator run.
verification_test: TE-LEGAL-08: query log by actor, subject token, category and time interval.

obligation: Components storing personal electronic health data support different retention periods and access rights that consider origins and categories.
derived_requirement: [INTERPRETATION] Evidence retention and access-control metadata must be policy-bound and category/origin-sensitive.
mechanism: interface specification privacy_controls.retention_boundary, policy_binding and semantic_binding; decision layer minimisation gate.
verification_test: TE-LEGAL-09: artefacts include retention/policy reference and no raw payload.

obligation: Natural persons can obtain information, including automatic notifications, on any access; information is free, without delay and available for at least three years from each access.
derived_requirement: [INTERPRETATION] TrustEvidence supports subject-facing access v2.0.0 development and notification feeds.
mechanism: interface specification verification API; IG validation verifier CapabilityStatement; A2/A3 proof paths for independent verifiability.
verification_test: TE-LEGAL-10: reconstruct access summary containing accessor, time/date and data category.

obligation: Emergency access to restricted electronic health data is logged clearly and understandably and is easily accessible for the data subject.
derived_requirement: [INTERPRETATION] Break-glass or override events require explicit evidence type, policy context and explanation.
mechanism: interface specification event_action, policy_binding, consent_binding and failure/outcome fields; IG validation AuditEvent outcome mapping.
verification_test: TE-LEGAL-11: override fixture includes policy version, override reason and verifier-visible explanation.

obligation: When personal electronic health data are registered or updated, EHRs identify the health professional, provider and time.
derived_requirement: [INTERPRETATION] Create/update provenance evidence binds actor, organisation and registration/update time.
mechanism: interface specification provenance_assertion; IG validation TEProvenance agent/entity mapping; formal definitions soundness/freshness.
verification_test: TE-LEGAL-12: W2/W3 provenance examples include actor, provider, action and recorded time.

obligation: The controller is responsible for, and must be able to demonstrate, compliance with Art. 5(1).
derived_requirement: [INTERPRETATION] Evidence supports accountability but does not establish lawfulness by itself.
mechanism: formal definitions soundness boundary; interface specification verifier API; legal traceability matrix; ledgers and evidence classes.
verification_test: TE-LEGAL-13: accountability report links artefact to actor, policy, proof status and verification result.

obligation: Personal data are adequate, relevant and limited to what is necessary.
derived_requirement: [INTERPRETATION] Store commitments/reference tokens rather than payloads or direct identifiers.
mechanism: interface specification subject_ref tokenisation, payload_binding commitment and privacy_controls; decision layer metadata gate.
verification_test: TE-LEGAL-14: reject raw payload or direct identifiers in backend-visible evidence.

obligation: Personal data are identifiable no longer than necessary, subject to safeguards for longer retention where applicable.
derived_requirement: [INTERPRETATION] Audit-evidence retention must be policy-bound and distinct from payload retention.
mechanism: interface specification retention boundary and policy_binding; decision layer proportionality screen.
verification_test: TE-LEGAL-15: artefact has retention-policy reference or explicit review flag.

obligation: Data protection by design and by default requires appropriate technical and organisational measures, including minimisation, considering risk, state of the art and cost.
derived_requirement: [INTERPRETATION] Backend choice must be property-, minimisation- and cost-aware.
mechanism: decision layer LJI 2.0 gate-first method; measurement protocol and external benchmark run cost slots; interface specification minimisation controls.
verification_test: TE-LEGAL-16: backend-design report justifies selected backend under named adversary and legal context.

obligation: Controllers and processors maintain records of processing activities with purposes, categories, recipients, transfers, erasure limits and security measures where applicable.
derived_requirement: [INTERPRETATION] TrustEvidence can support, not replace, the controller record of processing activities.
mechanism: interface specification semantic/policy/payload bindings; legal traceability export template; IG validation AuditEvent/Provenance mapping.
verification_test: TE-LEGAL-17: export evidence grouped by purpose, data category, actor/recipient token and retention policy.

obligation: Security of processing requires risk-appropriate measures including confidentiality, integrity, availability, resilience, recovery and regular testing.
derived_requirement: [INTERPRETATION] Legal traceability requires executable security tests, not a generic ledger claim.
mechanism: formal definitions/formal review property matrix; local implementation local tests; external-service integration external baseline harness; IG validation security considerations.
verification_test: TE-LEGAL-18: exercise tamper, rollback, fork, missing witness and verifier-state-loss tests.

obligation: ISO 27789 specifies a common EHR audit-trail framework to keep personal health information auditable across systems and domains.
derived_requirement: [INTERPRETATION] TrustEvidence is positioned as a verifiable evidence layer compatible with EHR audit-trail requirements.
mechanism: formal definitions/formal review properties; interface specification artefact core; draft FHIR IG validation AuditEvent/Provenance draft.
verification_test: TE-LEGAL-19: crosswalk each access/provenance event to an EHR-audit use case.

obligation: The public ISO abstract states applicability to systems creating secure audit records when a user reads, creates, updates or archives personal health information.
derived_requirement: [INTERPRETATION] W1-W5 workloads must cover read/access, create/update/provenance and archive/integrity-like events.
mechanism: measurement protocol workloads; interface specification event_action; IG validation examples; external benchmark run requires external validation execution.
verification_test: TE-LEGAL-20: validate workload coverage for read/create/update/archive-like events.

obligation: The ISO catalogue note states audit records at minimum identify user, subject of care, function performed and date/time.
derived_requirement: [INTERPRETATION] TrustEvidence artefacts encode or safely reference these fields.
mechanism: interface specification emitter, subject_ref, event_action and timestamps; IG validation AuditEvent agent/entity/action/recorded.
verification_test: TE-LEGAL-21: schema/examples reject missing actor, subject, function or time except failure artefact cases.

obligation: The ISO public abstract states audit records contain links to EHR segments as governed by access policy and exclude EHR personal health information beyond identifiers; system-management/security logs are outside scope.
derived_requirement: [INTERPRETATION] TrustEvidence must not become a duplicate clinical repository or generic SIEM log.
mechanism: Off-chain payload custody; interface specification payload commitments and reference tokens; IG validation privacy/security narrative.
verification_test: TE-LEGAL-22: inspect evidence to confirm no raw clinical payload and no unrelated telemetry.
